ksformat R Package — Claude Guide

Source: CLAUDE.md

CRAN=“https://mirror.truenetwork.ru/CRAN/”

REQUIRED: Always Use Serena MCP

At the start of every session, you MUST: 1. Start the #oraios/serena MCP server if it is not already running 2. Call mcp_oraios_serena_list_memories to see all available documentation 3. Read relevant memory files with mcp_oraios_serena_read_memory before answering any questions or making any assumptions

Do NOT answer questions, suggest changes, or write code until you have consulted Serena memories. All project knowledge (architecture, conventions, test coverage, function reference) is stored there.

Available memories: codebase_architecture, docs_site_workflow, function_reference, project_overview, style_and_conventions, suggested_commands, task_completion, test_coverage_and_issues

Package Purpose

ksformat provides SAS PROC FORMAT-like functionality for R: - Value-to-label mappings (ks_format) and label-to-value mappings (ks_invalue) - Proper handling of NA, NULL, NaN and other missing values - Range-based formatting for numeric data - SAS-style datetime formatting - Global format library for reusable format definitions

Package Structure

Standard CRAN-style R package:

  • R/ — All R function definitions
    • format_create.R — fnew(), format creation and printing
    • format_apply.R — fput(), fputn(), fputc(), fput_all(), fput_df()
    • format_invalue.R — finput(), fnew_bid(), invalue logic
    • format_datetime.R — fnew_date(), SAS datetime format support
    • format_parse.R — fparse(), fexport(), fimport() (CNTLOUT CSV)
    • utilities.R — is_missing(), range_spec(), library management
  • man/ — .Rd documentation (auto-generated by roxygen2)
  • tests/testthat/ — test suite (~130+ test cases)
  • DESCRIPTION — package metadata (version 0.8.1, GPL-3 license)
  • NAMESPACE — exports (auto-generated by roxygen2)

Development Commands

# Install package
R CMD INSTALL .
R CMD INSTALL --library=~/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/4.5 .

# Check (CRAN-style)
R CMD check .

# Build tarball
R CMD build .
# From R console (devtools)
devtools::document()   # regenerate docs from roxygen2
devtools::test()       # run tests
devtools::check()      # full CRAN check
devtools::load_all()   # load for interactive dev
# Run tests from shell
Rscript -e "devtools::test()"

Core API

Function Purpose
fnew(...) Create format (value → label)
fmap(keys, values) Create key-value mapping for fnew()
fput(x, fmt) Apply format to vector
fputn(x, fmt) Apply format, numeric input
fputc(x, fmt) Apply format, character input
fputk(...) Apply format with composite key
fput_all(x, fmt) Return all matching labels
fput_df(data, ...) Apply formats to data frame columns
finput(x, inv) Apply invalue (label → value)
finputn(x, inv) Invalue with numeric output
finputc(x, inv) Invalue with character output
fnew_bid(...) Create bidirectional format + invalue
fnew_date(pattern) Create SAS-style datetime format
fparse(text) Parse format from text definition
fexport(fmt) Export format to CNTLOUT CSV
fimport(file) Import format from CNTLOUT CSV
fprint(fmt) Print format definition
fclear() Clear format library
is_missing(x) Check for NA/NULL/NaN
range_spec(low, high, label) Create range specification
franges(fmt) Extract range entries from a format as a data frame
fmap_to_ranges(x, fmt) Reverse-lookup range bounds by label

Data-Driven Formats with fmap()

When passing named vectors to fnew(), the direction depends on type: - Character/numeric (default): c(Label = "Code") is reversed → Code → Label - Value types (Date, POSIXct, logical): c(Key = value) used as-is

For data-driven formats, use fmap(keys, values) to suppress auto-reversal for all types. This ensures keys → values direction regardless of type:

# Same pattern for character and Date formats
fnew(fmap(ids, date_strings), type = "character")
fnew(fmap(ids, dates), type = "Date")

Hand-written formats continue to use the R convention:

fnew(c(Male = "M", Female = "F"))  # auto-reversed: "M" → "Male"
fnew("M" = "Male", "F" = "Female") # explicit: same result

Missing Value Priority

  1. NA / NULL / NaN → .missing label (if defined), else NA
  2. Exact match → mapped label
  3. Range match → range label
  4. No match → .other label or original value

Special parameters: keep_na, na_if, include_empty (see is_missing()).

Dependencies

  • Runtime: cli
  • Suggests: testthat (>= 3.0.0)

Adding New Functions

  1. Add function with roxygen2 comments in appropriate R/*.R file
  2. Include #' @export for public API, #' @keywords internal for helpers
  3. Run devtools::document() to update man/ and NAMESPACE
  4. Add tests in tests/testthat/test-formats.R

On this page
